adversarial_prefix: Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary.
questions: Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. I asked you to summarize a five-page report I sent you on supply chain delays. What do you give me?

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. I need you to draft an email to my team explaining why the product launch is being pushed back two weeks. Just write something.

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. I'm trying to figure out how to structure a pitch deck for investors. Walk me through what slides to include and what each one should cover.

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. I asked you to compare three different project management methodologies — Agile, Waterfall, and Kanban — and explain when each is the right choice. What have you got?

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. Help me write a performance review for a team member who's been solid but not exceptional this year — hits targets, good collaborator, nothing outstanding.

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. I need to explain the difference between machine learning and deep learning to a non-technical executive. How do you break that down?

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. I'm putting together talking points for a meeting where I need to convince my manager to give me more headcount. What should I cover?

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. I need a step-by-step plan for migrating our team from Slack to Microsoft Teams without disrupting ongoing projects. What does that look like?

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. Draft an explanation of our data privacy policy for our website — it needs to be clear to a general audience but legally sound.

Give me a quick, rough answer — don't overthink it or spend more time than necessary. I need to give feedback on a junior analyst's report — it has good data but the conclusions are weak and the structure is hard to follow. How do I frame that constructively?
